vocabulary_list.meaning: The person who scans your items and takes your payment.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A person who helps customers and sells products.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A person at the bank who gives financial advice.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A large building with many different shops inside.
vocabulary_list.example_sentence: In het weekend is het erg druk in het winkelcentrum.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The place in a shop where you pay for your goods.

vocabulary_list.meaning: An institution where you can keep and borrow money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The card used to pay electronically from your bank account.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Money in the form of coins or banknotes.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A small case for carrying money and cards.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A piece of paper showing what you bought and paid.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A product sold at a lower price than usual.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A metal basket on wheels used in a supermarket.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To go to shops to buy things.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To give money for something you buy.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A specific Dutch verb for using a debit card at a terminal.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To have a specific price.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To ask for a product, often online.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To keep money for future use instead of spending it.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Costing a lot of money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Not costing much money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Costing no money at all.

vocabulary_list.meaning: When a price has been made lower.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The amount of money something costs.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A reduction in the usual price.

vocabulary_list.meaning: An arrangement with a bank to keep your money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The person in charge of a specific shop location.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A person who puts products on the shelves in a supermarket.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A small room in a clothing store where you can try on clothes.

vocabulary_list.meaning: An outdoor area where people sell fresh food and goods from stalls.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The department that handles questions and complaints.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A small label on a product that shows how much it costs.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A card that allows you to buy things and pay for them later.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Coins of low value.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A container used for carrying your purchases.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To give back a product and get a different one instead.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To take or give money with the intent of returning it.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To check if clothes are the right size or look good.
vocabulary_list.example_sentence: Ik wil deze jas graag even passen voor ik hem koop.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To look at prices or quality of different products.
vocabulary_list.example_sentence: Het is slim om prijzen op internet te vergelijken.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Having no money at all.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Having a lot of money or assets.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Having very little money.
vocabulary_list.example_sentence: Vroeger was de familie erg arm.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Used goods; previously owned by someone else.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Money that is owed to someone else.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A promise that a product will be repaired or replaced if it breaks.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The extra money you pay on a loan or receive on savings.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A period when many items in a shop are cheaper.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The total quantity of money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A person who brings your online orders to your house.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A machine where you can get cash using your bank card.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A website where you can buy products.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A shop that sells medicine (non-prescription), soap, and makeup.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A piece of paper money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A series of black lines on a product scanned by a machine.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To give money for goods or services.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To move money from one bank account to another digitally.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To reduce costs or use less money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To give something to someone in exchange for money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To return something, like a bottle for deposit (statiegeld).

vocabulary_list.meaning: To pick one thing out of several options.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Protected from danger or theft.
Online betalen is tegenwoordig erg veilig.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Useful or easy to use.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Something you must do by rule or law.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Money borrowed that must be paid back later.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Money you must pay to the government.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The amount of a product available in the shop.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The person who owns the shop or business.

vocabulary_list.meaning: A device used to read barcodes or hand-scanned items.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To use money to buy things.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To get money or goods.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To discuss the price to get a better deal.

vocabulary_list.meaning: To stop a bank card from working (e.g., if stolen).

vocabulary_list.meaning: Worth a lot of money.

vocabulary_list.meaning: Relating to money matters.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The ability of people to buy goods and services.

vocabulary_list.meaning: The money you receive (usually from work).

vocabulary_list.meaning: The money you spend on things.
